correctness.notes: Lemma feasible-vc-offset / Theorem feasible-upper-log: The reproduced second-moment bound for the feasible estimated score contains an estimated-clipping instability term absent from the note: for a disagreement set D, E[1_D Gammahat_q^2 | T] is not implied to be O(q^{-1})P_X(D) by L2 propensity control alone; without ||ehat-e||_infty<=c q it can include a term of order q^{-2} E[1_D |ehat-e| | T], yielding a localized stochastic radius sqrt(q^{-2}||ehat-e||_2 rho^{alpha/(2+2alpha)} log(e/rho)/n). The note instead asserts ehat_q>=e/2 on {e>2q} and keeps only the q^{-1} variance term, but Assumption nuisance states only an L2 propensity rate.; Lemma witness-membership / Theorem lower-full-class: The reproduced chi-square term is m_h * P(A=perturbed arm | B_h) * h^2. The note substitutes m_h q_h h^2=h^{2+alpha+beta} after concretely perturbing the treated arm, but Assumption primitive-cube only states min(e,1-e) asymp q_h on B_h; it does not require the sign-varying regression to be placed on the weak arm. If e asymp 1-q_h and the treated arm is perturbed, the term is m_h(1-q_h)h^2 asymp h^{2+alpha}, not h^{2+alpha+beta}.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Lemma feasible-vc-offset / Theorem feasible-upper-log: The reproduced second-moment bound for the feasible estimated score contains an estimated-clipping instability term absent from the note: for a disagreement set D, E[1_D Gammahat_q^2 | T] is not implied to be O(q^{-1})P_X(D) by L2 propensity control alone; without ||ehat-e||_infty<=c q it can include a term of order q^{-2} E[1_D |ehat-e| | T], yielding a localized stochastic radius sqrt(q^{-2}||ehat-e||_2 rho^{alpha/(2+2alpha)} log(e/rho)/n). The note instead asserts ehat_q>=e/2 on {e>2q} and keeps only the q^{-1} variance term, but Assumption nuisance states only an L2 propensity rate. → The displayed upper rate is not derived from the stated standard L2 nuisance assumptions; the missing term can dominate the claimed fixed point in part of the advertised beta<=2 regime unless an additional clipping-stability condition is imposed or a sharper L2-only argument changes the regime/rate.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Lemma witness-membership / Theorem lower-full-class: The reproduced chi-square term is m_h * P(A=perturbed arm | B_h) * h^2. The note substitutes m_h q_h h^2=h^{2+alpha+beta} after concretely perturbing the treated arm, but Assumption primitive-cube only states min(e,1-e) asymp q_h on B_h; it does not require the sign-varying regression to be placed on the weak arm. If e asymp 1-q_h and the treated arm is perturbed, the term is m_h(1-q_h)h^2 asymp h^{2+alpha}, not h^{2+alpha+beta}. → With the stated h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta)}, the Le Cam information would be n h_n^{2+alpha}=h_n^{-beta}, so the lower-bound indistinguishability step fails unless the witness assumption or proof explicitly perturbs whichever arm has probability min(e,1-e).

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[serious, setup] Lemma feasible-vc-offset / Theorem feasible-upper-log: The reproduced second-moment bound for the feasible estimated score contains an estimated-clipping instability term absent from the note: for a disagreement set D, E[1_D Gammahat_q^2 | T] is not implied to be O(q^{-1})P_X(D) by L2 propensity control alone; without ||ehat-e||_infty<=c q it can include a term of order q^{-2} E[1_D |ehat-e| | T], yielding a localized stochastic radius sqrt(q^{-2}||ehat-e||_2 rho^{alpha/(2+2alpha)} log(e/rho)/n). The note instead asserts ehat_q>=e/2 on {e>2q} and keeps only the q^{-1} variance term, but Assumption nuisance states only an L2 propensity rate.. Fix: The displayed upper rate is not derived from the stated standard L2 nuisance assumptions; the missing term can dominate the claimed fixed point in part of the advertised beta<=2 regime unless an additional clipping-stability condition is imposed or a sharper L2-only argument changes the regime/rate.
[serious, setup] Lemma witness-membership / Theorem lower-full-class: The reproduced chi-square term is m_h * P(A=perturbed arm | B_h) * h^2. The note substitutes m_h q_h h^2=h^{2+alpha+beta} after concretely perturbing the treated arm, but Assumption primitive-cube only states min(e,1-e) asymp q_h on B_h; it does not require the sign-varying regression to be placed on the weak arm. If e asymp 1-q_h and the treated arm is perturbed, the term is m_h(1-q_h)h^2 asymp h^{2+alpha}, not h^{2+alpha+beta}.. Fix: With the stated h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta)}, the Le Cam information would be n h_n^{2+alpha}=h_n^{-beta}, so the lower-bound indistinguishability step fails unless the witness assumption or proof explicitly perturbs whichever arm has probability min(e,1-e).

Referee notes: I reproduced the upper clipped-AIPW bias/VC fixed point and the lower two-point witness. The polynomial lower calibration is correct once the perturbed outcome arm is explicitly the weak arm, but the stated upper proof drops an estimated-clipping variance term not controlled by the advertised L2 nuisance assumptions.
